figures.caption: Figure 2: Simulation results for the estimation errors as the dimension d increases. The average errors for the direct and recursive estimators are shown in blue and red, with shaded bands representing the standard errors across the R = 1000 simulation repeats.

Figure 3: Simulation results for coverage probabilities and average interval length for the direct and recursive estimators, shown in blue and red, over R = 1000 simulation repeats at the 90% nominal target.

Figure 4: Validation forecasts for later-treated cities after their first home game with fans. The solid black line shows observed COVID-19 case counts, while the dash-dotted red line shows forecasts from the TWSF estimator, constructed using only data available up to the target city’s opening date. Shaded bands denote pointwise 90% confidence intervals. The first dotted vertical line marks September 10, when Kansas City first admitted fans, and hence the end of the pre-treatment window. The second dotted vertical line marks September 21, the first day after all cities in the treated donor pool I1 had admitted fans, and hence the start of the post-treatment window used to learn the temporal dynamics. The final dotted vertical line marks the target city’s actual opening date; forecasts are then produced for the following 14 days and compared with the realized trajectory.

Figure 5: Counterfactual forecasts for control cities under the hypothetical policy that they admitted fans at their first home game after October 1, 2020. The solid black line shows observed COVID-19 case counts, while the dash-dotted red line shows forecasts from the TWSF estimator. Shaded bands denote pointwise 90% confidence intervals. The first dotted vertical line marks September 10, when Kansas City first admitted fans, and hence the end of the pre-treatment window. The second dotted vertical line marks September 21, the first day after all cities in the treated donor pool I1 had admitted fans, and hence the start of the post-treatment window used to learn the temporal dynamics. The final dotted vertical line marks the target city’s hypothetical opening date; forecasts are then produced for the following 14 days.
